queries.query: we're shipping a 1.0 of a public SDK for our payments API in python and node next quarter. what api contracts / surface decisions should i pin down before 1.0 so we don't end up with a hyrum's-law nightmare a year in?
i want to design the error envelope for our REST API from scratch. what shape should it take if i want clients to handle retries and idempotency cleanly?
design how our cli reads config — file vs env vs flag precedence, where the config file lives on linux/mac/windows, and how we validate. what should we get right up front?
we're designing a new HTTP API for billing events. what should we get right up front so the contract holds up?
design the plugin api for our cli so third parties can register subcommands without forking the binary. similar to how kubectl plugins or npx scripts get wired
we don't have a samples directory and our docs only have inline snippets. before launch, what should a proper examples surface look like for an SDK shipping in 3 languages?
designing the first-run setup for a new dev tool — what should the install path and first-run experience be so a new developer is productive in minutes?
we're planning the deprecation lifecycle and upgrade path for a breaking v2 — how should we design the migration so existing integrators aren't stranded?
ok our cli's --help output is dense and new joiners can't tell --region from --zone. take a pass at the existing help layout and tell me what to tighten
customer keeps hitting `auth failed` after rotating api keys. they swear they're using the new key. what should we look at?
do a 30-second basics pass on this repo — does it have a working README install snippet, a LICENSE, --help and --version working
design a new homepage hero section for our marketing site — clear value prop for general buyers, not devs
our consumer signup form has a 60% drop-off. help me design a lower-friction 3-step flow
design the agent loop and tool-call contract for a python AI/Agent SDK with stopWhen-style predicates and pre-tool approval hooks
refactor this typescript function to use early returns instead of nested if statements
